articles.title: BP, Walmart and 7-Eleven sued over AI-set petrol prices in California
articles.description: California drivers have sued BP, Walmart, 7-Eleven and three other chains, alleging an AI pricing tool was used to coordinate higher petrol prices.

articles.title: Worst is over for crude oil prices for now: Jim Burkhard, S&P Global Energy
articles.description: Oil prices outlook: S&P Global sees Brent averaging $80-$90 a barrel in H2CY26 as supply recovers slowly after the Hormuz disruption, with China's buying patterns holding the key.

articles.title: Oil prices hit lows not seen since early March on US-Iran peace talk progress
articles.description: Brent crude dropped nearly 2% to 76.5 US dollars a barrel at one stage in morning trading on Tuesday.

articles.title: Asian shares mostly slip amid caution about the war in Iran
articles.description: Asian shares are mostly lower as recent enthusiasm cools and markets face uncertainty about efforts to end the war in Iran. Benchmarks fell Tuesday in Tokyo, Sydney, Seoul, Hong Kong and Shanghai. Oil prices momentarily rose but fell later in the day in Asia. An end to the war could open the Strait of Hormuz for oil tankers and allow for the full resumption of deliveries from the Persian Gulf. U.S. stocks drifted through a mixed day of trading after Big Tech stocks weighed on Wall Street. The S&P 500 slipped 0.4%, the Dow added 0.3%, and the Nasdaq slumped 1.3%.

articles.title: Prime Day poised to set online spending record
articles.description: Shoppers feeling the sting of higher gas prices are expected to snatch up deals on back-to-school clothes and household goods, pushing online spending to another record during Amazon.com Inc.'s 12th annual Prime Day sale that begins Tuesday.

articles.title: US Iran truce, lower crude to drive market; Medanta, GMR Airport, Mankind midcaps to buy
articles.description: Experts say easing oil prices may benefit financials, autos and realty, while Emkay sees sustained midcap outperformance through 2027.

articles.title: Rupee flat as traders mull Fed rate path, keep eyes peeled for flow clues
articles.description: The Indian rupee held steady as falling oil prices provided some relief, though traders remained cautious due to anticipated US Federal Reserve rate hikes. While policy measures have helped the rupee recover, the prospect of higher US interest rates poses a challenge. Analysts are closely watching the impact of these measures on dollar inflows, with some predicting significant amounts.
